q: The speed of sound depends on the temperature of the medium.
Sound waves travel faster in solids than in gases.
The Doppler effect occurs only when the source of sound is moving.
When a source of sound approaches a stationary observer, the observed frequency increases.
The frequency of sound is measured in units of Hertz (Hz).
In a longitudinal wave, particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of wave motion.
Sound waves are electromagnetic waves.
The pitch of a sound is determined primarily by its frequency.
Ultrasound has a frequency lower than the audible range for humans.
The audible range of frequency for the human ear is roughly 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz.
When the observer moves away from a stationary source, the observed frequency becomes smaller.
The speed of sound in air at 0°C is approximately 331 m/s.
Echoes are produced by the reflection of sound waves.
The intensity of sound is related to the amplitude of the sound wave.
Beats are produced by the interference of two sound waves of slightly different frequencies.
A stationary observer perceives a lower frequency when a source moves away.
Sound cannot travel through a vacuum.
The speed of sound is independent of the density of the medium.
Diffraction of sound is the bending of sound waves around obstacles.
Ultrasound can be used to examine internal organs like the heart and kidney.
The Doppler effect is used in police radar to measure the speed of cars.
If the observer and the source move in the same direction at the same speed, there is no Doppler shift.
Resonance occurs when the frequency of forced vibrations matches the natural frequency of an object.
The quality or timbre of sound allows us to distinguish between different musical instruments.
Infrasound refers to sound waves with frequencies above 20,000 Hz.
Sound waves in air are transverse waves.
The wavelength of sound increases as the frequency decreases, assuming speed is constant.
A decibel (dB) is a unit used to measure sound intensity level.
Ultrasonic waves can help reveal blockages to blood flow in arteries.
The loudness of sound is purely an objective physical quantity.
Mach number is the ratio of the speed of an object to the speed of sound.
Shock waves are produced when an object moves faster than the speed of sound.
The fundamental frequency is the lowest frequency of a vibrating object.
Harmonics are integral multiples of the fundamental frequency.
Standing waves are formed by the superposition of two waves of the same frequency traveling in opposite directions.
An open pipe has antinodes at both ends.
A pipe closed at one end can produce all even and odd harmonics.
A pipe closed at one end produces only odd harmonics.
The speed of sound in water is higher than the speed of sound in air.
Sound intensity decreases as the square of the distance from the source.
Echocardiography uses ultrasound to create images of the heart.
The Doppler effect applies to light waves as well as sound waves.
The frequency of a sound wave changes when it passes from one medium to another.
The speed of sound increases with an increase in humidity.
Sonic boom is the sound associated with shock waves.
Bats use ultrasound for navigation and locating prey.
The threshold of hearing is defined as 0 dB.
Compression is a region in a longitudinal wave where the particles are closest together.
Rarefaction is a region where the density and pressure are minimum.
Nodes are points in a standing wave where the displacement is maximum.
